§6Combination Oven§f

§aHeat-based processor that cokes materials and produces liquid byproducts — your earliest source of wood oil and coal tar!§f

The §bCombination Oven§f is a unique furnace that uses heat exchange to transform items while producing liquid byproducts. It requires a §bheat source§f from below (Firebox, Heating Oven, Electric Heater, or Fluid Burner) and produces a column of fire above itself during operation.

§6Crafting§f (Tier 2 Anvil):
§b• §e8×§f Stone Bricks
§b• §e16×§f Oak Wood
§b• §e2×§f Cast Copper Plate
§b• §e16×§f Brick

§6Key Recipes§f:
§b• Coal Briquette§f → §e1×§f Coal Coke + §e150mB§f Coal Tar Creosote.
§b• Lignite Briquette§f → §e1×§f Lignite Coke + §e100mB§f Coal Tar Creosote.
§b• Coal Powder§f → §e1×§f Coal Coke + §e100mB§f Coal Tar Creosote.
§b• Lignite Powder§f → §e1×§f Lignite Coke + §e50mB§f Coal Tar Creosote.
§b• Oil Tar / Crack Oil Tar§f → §bPetroleum Coke§f + byproducts.
§b• Wood / Sawdust Briquettes§f → §bCharcoal§f + §bWood Oil§f.

§eTip§f: The Combination Oven can be made self-sufficient by piping the liquid output (Wood Oil or Coal Tar Creosote) into a §bFluid Burner§f to heat the oven. Use a §bHeating Oven§f for faster operation — its larger heat buffer prevents interruptions during long processing runs!
